summary: Provider module-level languages/sdk Maps grow unboundedly
details: Module-level `languages` Map (line 1221) and `sdk` Map (line 1228) inside Layer.effect grow with every unique model SDK resolved and never evict. No size cap, no LRU, no weak references. Process lifetime leak.

summary: Explanation event cache has no callers for clear/invalidate
details: Module-level cache Map (line 50) grows with every unique eventId explained. clearExplanationCache() and invalidateExplanation() are exported but never called from any consumer. No eviction policy exists.

summary: forkDaemon in context/packet without managed scope creates immortal fiber
details: forkDaemon spawns an immortal fiber that listens to invalidation bus. If the layer is rebuilt or re-initialized, old daemon fibers accumulate. No interrupt path for previous daemon fibers exists.

summary: DuckDB subprocess listeners never cleaned up via .off()
details: stdout/stderr/close/error event listeners on DuckDB child process are set but never removed with proc.off(). On reconnection or hot-reload, listeners accumulate causing duplicate handling and memory pressure.

summary: Heap auto-snapshot timer has no clearInterval path
details: setInterval for heap monitoring is created but never stored with a clearInterval call path. On module re-import, duplicate intervals accumulate. Module-level `timer` variable is scoped to the start() closure but no stop() or cleanup function exists.

summary: bus-event registry grows monotonically without cleanup
details: Module-level `registry` Map stores all event type definitions. While bounded by known event types at current scale, hot-reload cycles could cause duplicate registration entries that are never cleaned up.
